page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn8te.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6462C processor running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration utilizes a single thread per core design, ensuring dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading overhead.

The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is delivered via four NVIDIA Ada Lovelace architecture GPUs, which provide a combined total of 192 GB of VRAM. This hardware profile does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached options.

Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 64 Gbps. The high density of dedicated CPU cores and GPU accelerators provides a consolidated hardware profile for resource-intensive deployments. Given this configuration, the instance is designed for workloads such as deep learning, GPU-accelerated data science, large-scale rendering, and complex parallel computing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn8te.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for high-performance parallel computing. Powered by the KVM hypervisor, it features 64 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 6462C processor operating at 3.3 GHz with an x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, translating to 8.0 GB of memory per core. Graphics and compute acceleration are provided by four NVIDIA Ada Lovelace GPUs, delivering a total of 192 GB of VRAM. The instance does not include local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions, but offers a high baseline network bandwidth of 64 Gbps. This hardware configuration is suited for GPU-intensive workloads, including machine learning model training, large-scale data science, and 3D rendering.

tagline: A high-performance GPU instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and four NVIDIA Ada Lovelace GPUs for intensive parallel computing workloads.
